msg: Local ~/.claude/skills/checkpoint was already a real non-symlink dir with a NEWER SKILL.md than repo; preserved it instead of clobbering with older repo copy per plan cp step

Plan's single-commit bootstrap (git add -A && commit once) breaks phantom check: since=commit itself gives empty diff, falsely flagging clean/ as phantoms; reconstructed a two-commit history (baseline stubs -> impl) via a committed .baseline/ dir.

Plan's fresh-clone detection via 'git -C dir log -1' is unreliable: it walks up to the parent repo and falsely succeeds, so bootstrap never runs; switched detection to test -d dir/.git plus toplevel==dir match.

Plan expected no-code-tag/ to yield 'findings', but phantom check never exits non-zero and the tagged tasks are correctly SKIPPED with the one code task implemented; asserted status=clean + docs_only_skipped=2 (the # no-code tag working) instead.
install.sh failed on clean HOME because it never created ~/.claude/skills and agents dirs before ln -s; added mkdir -p
